title: Urachne thomasii
intro: Urachne thomasii, also known as Thomas's Stickgrass, is a plant species in the family Poaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis. The bioactive constituents of Urachne thomasii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Thomas's Stickgrass is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in the production of extracts and tinctures for the treatment of various ailments.
taxonomy: Urachne thomasii is a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae. It is a type of grass, and its classification places it in the subfamily Pooideae. The plant is native to tropical Africa, specifically in countries such as South Africa and Madagascar. It is also known to be a part of the genus Urachne, which includes several species of grasses. Further classification of Urachne thomasii may include its specific rank and order within the Poaceae family.
